---
title: "ONE_DC_Script"
author: "Cindy Audiger"
date: "2026-07-01"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}

library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

```


```{r}
Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim <- FindNeighbors(Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim, dims = 1:20)
Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim <- FindClusters(Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim, resolution = 0.5, cluster.name = "NEW_CLUSTERS")
Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim <- RunUMAP(Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim, dims = 1:20, reduction.name = "umap_NEW_CLUSTERS")
```

#Panel A
```{r}
DimPlot(Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim, reduction = "umap_NEW_CLUSTERS")+
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1)
```


```{r}
p <- DimPlot(
  Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim,
  reduction = "umap_NEW_CLUSTERS"
)

ggsave(
  "UMAP_NEW_CLUSTERS.pdf",
  p,
  width = 6,
  height = 6
)
```


# Supplementary figure - PANEL A 
```{r}
DefaultAssay(Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim) <- "RNA"
Idents(Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim) <- "NEW_CLUSTERS"

Fresh_unstim_FindAllMarkers <- FindAllMarkers(Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim, only.pos = TRUE)

saveRDS(Fresh_unstim_FindAllMarkers, file="Fresh_unstim_FindAllMarkers.rds")

Fresh_unstim_FindAllMarkers %>%
group_by(cluster) %>%
dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 1)
Fresh_unstim_FindAllMarkers %>%
group_by(cluster) %>%
dplyr::filter(avg_log2FC > 1) %>%
slice_head(n = 20) %>%
ungroup() -> Fresh_unstim_FindAllMarkers_TOP20

DoHeatmap(object = Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim, assay="RNA", features = Fresh_unstim_FindAllMarkers_TOP20$gene,
group.by="NEW_CLUSTERS",size = 2 ) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 15),
axis.text.y = element_text(size = 4), aspect.ratio=50/30)

```



```{r}
seurat_obj     <- Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim   # object name in environment
CLUSTER_COL    <- "NEW_CLUSTERS"                     # metadata column
CLUSTERS_BLOOD <- c("g4")                            # blood DC1
CLUSTERS_CULT  <- c("g3", "g1", "g9")               # culture-derived DC1
N_HVG          <- 2000                               # HVGs to use for correlation
OUTDIR         <- "."                                # output directory
```




# PANEL B
```{r}
features <- c("CLEC9A", "IRF8", "CADM1","SIRPA", "IRF4", "ITGAX", "LAMP3", "CCR7", "MKI67", "MPO", "HBB", "CLC", "CD14", "XCR1", "CD83" )

# ── 2. Colour scale ───────────────────────────────────────────
# Light grey for zero expression, deep blue for high — clean and publication standard
#low_col  <- "#E8E8E8"
#high_col <- "#08306B"
 
# ── 3. Generate individual plots ─────────────────────────────
plot_list <- lapply(features, function(gene) {
  FeaturePlot(
    seurat_obj,
    features   = gene,
    reduction  = "umap_NEW_CLUSTERS",
    pt.size    = 0.8,
    order      = TRUE      # plot expressing cells on top
  ) +
  #scale_color_gradient(
    #low    = low_col,
    #high   = high_col,
    #name   = "Expression",
    #guide  = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 0.8, barheight = 6)
  #) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", name = "Expression",
                       guide = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 0.8, barheight = 6)) +
  labs(title = gene) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(
      face   = "bold.italic",
      size   = 10,
      hjust  = 0.5,
      margin = margin(b = 2)
    ),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position  = "right",
    plot.margin      = margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    axis.line        = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4),
    aspect.ratio     = 1
  )
})
 
# ── 4. Assemble 2-column layout with patchwork ───────────────
n_genes  <- length(features)
n_cols   <- 3
n_rows   <- ceiling(n_genes / n_cols)
 
combined <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = n_cols) +
  plot_layout(ncol = n_cols, widths = rep(1, n_cols)) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Marker gene expression",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0.5,
                                 margin = margin(b = 5))
    )
  ) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 0.2))
 
# ── 5. Save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Height scales with number of rows
# Square panels: width per panel = height per panel
panel_size  <- 2.8
plot_width  <- n_cols * panel_size
plot_height <- n_rows * panel_size
 
ggsave(
  file.path(OUTDIR, "FeaturePlot_markers_2col2.pdf"),
  combined,
  width  = plot_width,
  height = plot_height,
  device = "pdf"
)
 
cat("Saved: FeaturePlot_markers_2col2.pdf\n")
cat("Layout:", n_cols, "columns x", n_rows, "rows\n")
```



# Supplementary figure - PANEL C 
```{r}
# ── 0. Parameters ────────────────────────────────────────────
seurat_obj     <- Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim   # object name in environment
CLUSTER_COL    <- "NEW_CLUSTERS"                     # metadata column
CLUSTERS_BLOOD <- c("g4")                            # blood DC1
CLUSTERS_CULT  <- c("g3", "g1", "g9")               # culture-derived DC1
CLUSTER_NEG      <- c("g11")
N_HVG          <- 2000                               # HVGs to use for correlation
OUTDIR         <- "."                                # output directory
 
# ── 1. Load object ────────────────────────────────────────────
# If the object is already loaded in your session, replace this line:
#   seurat_obj <- Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim
# Otherwise load from file:
#   seurat_obj <- readRDS("path/to/Seurat_Join_Filt_fresh_unstim.rds")
#seurat_obj <- get(seurat_obj)
 
# Confirm cluster column exists
stopifnot(CLUSTER_COL %in% colnames(seurat_obj@meta.data))
Idents(seurat_obj) <- CLUSTER_COL
 
all_clusters <- sort(unique(as.character(seurat_obj@meta.data[[CLUSTER_COL]])))
cat("Clusters found:", paste(all_clusters, collapse = ", "), "\n")
 
# ── 2. Select HVGs from the normalised data ───────────────────
# VariableFeatures() works for both Seurat v4 (Assay) and v5 (Assay5)
hvg <- VariableFeatures(seurat_obj)
if (length(hvg) == 0) {
  message("No HVGs found — running FindVariableFeatures() now.")
  seurat_obj <- FindVariableFeatures(seurat_obj, nfeatures = N_HVG)
  hvg <- VariableFeatures(seurat_obj)
}
hvg <- hvg[seq_len(min(N_HVG, length(hvg)))]
cat("Using", length(hvg), "HVGs for correlation.\n")
 
# ── 3. Compute pseudobulk average expression per cluster ──────
# AverageExpression returns log-normalised mean per cluster
avg_expr <- AverageExpression(
  seurat_obj,
  features    = hvg,
  assays      = "RNA",
  group.by    = CLUSTER_COL,
  layer       = "data"     # uses normalised (log1p) counts
)$RNA
 
# Coerce to matrix and clean column names
avg_mat <- as.matrix(avg_expr)
colnames(avg_mat) <- as.character(colnames(avg_mat))
cat("Average expression matrix:", nrow(avg_mat), "genes ×", ncol(avg_mat), "clusters\n")
 

# ── 4. Compute Pearson correlation across clusters ────────────
cor_mat <- cor(avg_mat, method = "pearson")
 
# ── 5. Annotate clusters for the heatmap ─────────────────────
# Build a colour annotation bar: blood DC1 / culture DC1 / other
ann_df <- data.frame(
  Population = ifelse(
    colnames(cor_mat) %in% CLUSTERS_BLOOD, "Blood DC1",
    ifelse(colnames(cor_mat) %in% CLUSTERS_CULT, "Culture DC1",
    ifelse(colnames(cor_mat) %in% CLUSTER_NEG, "Blood DC2", "Other"))
  ),
  row.names = colnames(cor_mat)
)
 
ann_colours <- list(
  Population = c(
    "Blood DC1"        = "#CC79A7",
    "Culture DC1"      = "#9990FF",
    "Blood DC2" = "#FF9666",
    "Other"       = "#888780"    # gray
  )
)
 
# ── 6. Plot full correlation heatmap (all clusters) ───────────
# Reorder so DC1 clusters are together for easier reading
dc1_clusters   <- c(CLUSTERS_BLOOD, CLUSTERS_CULT, CLUSTER_NEG)  # added CLUSTER_NEG
other_clusters <- setdiff(colnames(cor_mat), dc1_clusters)
col_order      <- c(dc1_clusters, other_clusters)
cor_ordered    <- cor_mat[col_order, col_order]
 
pheatmap(
  cor_ordered,
  color            = colorRampPalette(c("#F5EBF0", "#CC79A7", "#663366"))(100),
  breaks           = seq(0.7, 1, length.out = 101),
  annotation_col   = ann_df,
  annotation_colors = ann_colours,
  annotation_row   = ann_df,
  cluster_rows     = TRUE,
  cluster_cols     = TRUE,
  show_rownames    = TRUE,
  show_colnames    = TRUE,
  fontsize         = 10,
  fontsize_row     = 9,
  fontsize_col     = 9,
  border_color     = NA,
  main             = "Inter-cluster transcriptional correlation (Pearson, HVGs)",
  filename         = file.path(OUTDIR, "DC1_correlation_heatmap_all_clusters.pdf"),
  width            = 8, height = 7
)
 
cat("Saved: DC1_correlation_heatmap_all_clusters.pdf\n")
 
```




# PANEL C
```{r}
# ── 7. Focused heatmap: DC1 clusters + Blood DC2 ─────────────
CLUSTER_NEG      <- "g11"   # blood DC2

# dc1_clusters already includes CLUSTER_NEG (from Panel A) — use it directly
focused_clusters <- unique(c(dc1_clusters, CLUSTER_NEG))  # unique() as a safety net

dc1_cor <- cor_mat[focused_clusters, focused_clusters]

ann_dc1 <- data.frame(
  Population = ifelse(
    focused_clusters %in% CLUSTERS_BLOOD, "Blood DC1",
    ifelse(focused_clusters %in% CLUSTERS_CULT, "Culture DC1", "Blood DC2")
  ),
  row.names = focused_clusters
)

ann_colours_focused <- list(
  Population = c(
    "Blood DC1"   = "#CC79A7",
    "Culture DC1" = "#9990FF",
    "Blood DC2"   = "#FF9666"
  )
)

pheatmap(
  dc1_cor,
  color             = colorRampPalette(c("#F5EBF0", "#CC79A7", "#663366"))(100),
  breaks            = seq(0.7, 1, length.out = 101),
  annotation_col    = ann_dc1,
  annotation_row    = ann_dc1,
  annotation_colors = ann_colours_focused,
  cluster_rows      = FALSE,
  cluster_cols      = FALSE,
  display_numbers   = TRUE,
  number_format     = "%.3f",
  number_color      = "black",
  fontsize          = 11,
  border_color      = NA,
  main              = "Blood DC1, Blood DC2 & Culture DC1 — pairwise correlation",
  filename          = file.path(OUTDIR, "DC1_correlation_heatmap_DC1_BloodDC2.pdf"),
  width             = 6, height = 5.5
)

cat("Saved: DC1_correlation_heatmap_DC1_BloodDC2.pdf\n")
```
